title: Incursa.Quic Stream Capacity Callback Verification
verification_method: Reflection checks, public API diff inspection, and focused loopback tests against the existing runtime seam and supported active-loopback MAX_STREAMS path.
preconditions: The public API analyzer files are available for comparison.
The loopback establishment helpers can create a supported client/server pair.
scope: This slice covers the public callback surface, the initial peer stream-capacity delta on the supported loopback path, later real peer `MAX_STREAMS` growth on the supported active loopback path, real peer stream-close-driven capacity release on the supported active loopback path, silence when the boundary is not reached, zero-delta silence, and cancellation/disposal honesty. It does not prove write-heavy stream behavior, reset/stop-sending parity, 0-RTT, key update, or interop-runner enablement.
expected_result: The public API includes QuicStreamCapacityChangedArgs and QuicConnectionOptions.StreamCapacityCallback, the supported loopback path reports the initial peer delta exactly once, later real peer `MAX_STREAMS` growth is reported truthfully, real peer stream-close-driven capacity release is reported truthfully, the callback stays silent when it should, and the public/internal boundary remains clean.
procedure: Compare src/Incursa.Quic/PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt against the approved public surface to confirm the callback type and property are present once.
Run the requirement-home shape tests for REQ-QUIC-API-0001 and REQ-QUIC-API-0005.
Run the supported loopback positive callback test in REQ-QUIC-API-0009.
Run the later MAX_STREAMS growth, stream-close-driven release, and zero-delta suppression tests in REQ-QUIC-API-0009.
Run the cancellation/disposal tests that keep the callback silent when it should not fire.
Inspect the connection/runtime seam to confirm the callback is backed by existing transport-parameter commit bookkeeping and the supported active-loopback MAX_STREAMS parse path.
status_summary: Execution evidence is expected to cover the initial peer stream-capacity callback delta, later real MAX_STREAMS growth, real peer stream-close-driven capacity release, zero-delta suppression, and cancellation/disposal honesty. Broader write-heavy stream behavior remains out of scope.
